days.title: Introduction
tasks: # Backend Engineering Mastery Plan — 2026 Edition
### Focus: DBMS Internals · Distributed Systems · Backend 2026 Roadmap
### Target: Infraspec · AI-First Product Engineering · Backend at Scale

days.title: Curriculum
tasks: ### Stage 1 — Fundamentals
| Concept | Covered By |
| HTTP/HTTPS — Methods, Status Codes, Headers, HTTP/2, TLS | All APIs |
| DNS — A, CNAME, MX, TXT, TTL, Resolution Path | Biweekly 04 (resolver) |
| TCP/UDP — Handshake, Time_Wait, Window Scaling | Biweekly 02 (pgpool) |
### Stage 5 — DBMS Deep (The Priority)
| Concept | Covered By |
| WAL (Write-Ahead Log) — Durability, Checkpoints, fsync | Biweekly 01 (vault) |
| MVCC (Multi-Version Concurrency Control) — Readers don't block writers | PayCore (Ledger) |
| B-Tree vs LSM-Tree — Split/Merge, Sequential vs Random I/O | Biweekly 05 (lsm) |
| Connection Pooling — Transaction vs Session Mode | Biweekly 02 (pgpool) |
| Indexing — GIN, BRIN, Partial, Composite column order | RouteMaster (Elasticsearch) |
### Stage 14 — Distributed Systems Deep
| Concept | Covered By |
| Leader election — Redis SETNX + TTL + heartbeat | DungBeetle (cron leader), distributed locking logic |
| Distributed locks with fencing tokens | BookWise (seat lock), PayCore (idempotency), lsm (compaction lock) |
| Consistent hashing + virtual nodes | DungBeetle (job routing), OpenTrace (trace routing), RouteMaster (driver assignment) |
| Bloom filters — false positive rate, Redis BF | RouteMaster (web crawler dedup), lsm (per-SSTable) |
| CAP theorem — CP vs AP tradeoffs | All ADRs |
| Saga pattern — choreography + compensating transactions | PayCore, BookWise |
| Event sourcing — double-entry ledger | PayCore (transactions) |
### Stage 15 — Engineering Practices
| Concept | Covered By |
| RFC / ADR — One document per major decision | All 13 repositories |
| OTel — Tracing, Metrics, Logs | OpenTrace (native), All others (instrumented) |
| CI/CD — Lint, Test (Race), Build, Deploy | GitHub Actions (all repos) |
| Zero Seq Scans — Index-only hot paths | PostgreSQL `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` requirement |

days.title: Curriculum
tasks: ### A Full Distributed Tracing System — Built Like Jaeger

OpenTrace is your final year project and your portfolio centrepiece. It is a complete open-source distributed tracing system — built in Go (backend) and TypeScript (frontend) — that mirrors Jaeger's architecture from the ground up.
| Component | Language | Mirrors | Built In |
| **Collector** | Go | Jaeger Collector | Month 2 |
| **Pipeline Processor** | Go | Jaeger Ingester | Month 3 |
| **Storage Layer** | Go | ClickHouse Plugin | Month 5 |
| **Query Service** | Go | Jaeger Query Service | Month 6 |
| **API Gateway** | TypeScript | Switchboard Gateway | Month 6 |
| **OpenTrace UI** | TypeScript/React | Jaeger UI | Month 4 + 7 |
| **SDK** | Go + TypeScript | OTel SDK | Month 7 |

days.title: Curriculum
tasks: | Project | Role | Stack | What It Demonstrates |
| **OpenTrace** | Portfolio Centrepiece | Go + TypeScript + ClickHouse + Kafka | Full distributed tracing system |
| **PayCore** | Financial Ledger | Go + PostgreSQL + Redis + Kafka | Idempotency, double-entry bookkeeping, Saga pattern |
| **DungBeetle** | Background Job Platform | Go + Kafka + PostgreSQL + Redis | Leader election, exactly-once cron, worker pools |
| **BookWise** | Distributed Booking | Go + PostgreSQL + Redis + Kafka | Distributed locking, double-booking prevention |
| **RouteMaster** | Logistics & Notifications | Next.js + Go + Elasticsearch + Kafka | Fan-out notifications, full-text search, tracking |

title: 8–9 MONTH OVERVIEW — ALL 5 PROJECTS + BACKEND 2026 ROADMAP

days.title: Curriculum
tasks: | Month | Backend 2026 Focus | OpenTrace Milestone | Other Projects (Independent) |
| **1** | Fundamentals + OS | OTLP Study, Proto definitions | Initialise 5 separate repos |
| **2** | Node.js + API Design | **Collector v0.1**: OTLP receiver | PayCore: Auth + Idempotency |
| **3** | Go Concurrency | **Pipeline**: Kafka consumer | DungBeetle: Job queue in Go |
| **4** | React + Next.js UI | **OpenTrace UI v0.1**: Waterfall | RouteMaster: Notifications |
| **5** | **DBMS Deep** | **Storage**: ClickHouse schema | PayCore: Ledger + Event sourcing |
| **6** | Infra + K8s | **Query Service**: gRPC streaming | BookWise: Reservation engine |
| **7** | **Dist. Systems Deep** | **OpenTrace UI v2 + SDK** | DungBeetle: Cron + HA Leader |
| **8** | Perf + AI Stack | **10M spans/sec load test** | AI features in all projects |
| **9** | Polish + LFX Prep | **LFX Application final** | Portfolio READMEs + Demos live |

title: BIWEEKLY PROJECTS — DATABASE + DISTRIBUTED SYSTEMS INTERNALS

days.title: Curriculum
tasks: | # | Name | Language | What It Teaches |
| 01 | **`vault`** | Go | WAL, crash recovery, log compaction |
| 02 | **`pgpool`** | Go | pgwire protocol, connection multiplexing |
| 03 | **`relay`** | Go | Redis pub/sub fan-out, distributed presence |
| 04 | **`resolver`** | Go | UDP DNS, binary protocol parsing, TTL cache |
| 05 | **`lsm`** | Go | MemTable, SSTable, Bloom filters, compaction |
| 06 | **`herald`** | TypeScript | Reliable delivery, outbox, multi-channel |
| 07 | **`gatekeeper`** | TypeScript | OTP lifecycle, hashed storage, rate limiting |
| 08 | **`switchboard`** | TypeScript | API gateway, JWT RS256, circuit breakers |

days.title: Curriculum
tasks: BookWise is a production-grade distributed booking platform that handles 10K concurrent booking attempts with zero double-bookings.
**Core Mechanisms:**
- **Atomic Inventory**: Redis `DECR` with PostgreSQL fallback.
- **Distributed Locks**: Fencing tokens via Biweekly 07 (gatekeeper/mutex patterns).
- **Waitlist State Machine**: Kafka-driven automatic assignment when a booking is cancelled.
- **Payment Saga**: Reserve seat → Charge card → Confirm (compensating transactions if failed).

days.title: Curriculum
tasks: | Block | Duration | Activity |
| **Morning** | 2 hours | **Concept Deep Dive**: Read source code (not docs). Break things. |
| **Evening** | 2 hours | **Build**: Ship a named feature in OpenTrace, PayCore, or others. |
| **DSA** | 30 min | **Logic Ingestion**: 1 problem connected to systems reality. |
| **RFC/ADR** | 30 min | **Communication**: Document every major technical decision. |
| **Weekend** | 8 hours | **Capstone**: Wire everything together, benchmark with k6, and deploy. |
